Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute
At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value
Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span
Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input

prompt_summary: Create a messy, production-real ashby-style job application (single-page, large, tier=high). Widgets: combobox. Required: Yes/no question as paired button elements, not native radio inputs Required: Label wraps input through nested divs without for attribute Required: At least one readonly or disabled input with prefilled profile value Required: Field labeled via aria-labelledby pointing at hidden or distant span Required: Custom text field: div with role=textbox and contenteditable, no native input Reference DOM (Collapsible section animation): Additional questions in collapsible section toggled by aria-expanded button Reference DOM (Radio styled as buttons): Native radios hidden; visible pill buttons styled via CSS class Reference DOM (Meter self assessment): Self-assessment field uses meter element near text inputs Do NOT produce a tutorial-style form where every field is label[for]+native input. Mix native controls with non-semantic production DOM from the patterns below. Implement ALL required complexity patterns: pill-yes-no, label-wraps-deep, readonly-prefill, aria-labelledby-hidden, div-role-textbox. Include at least one custom widget (div role=textbox, pill buttons, or listbox combobox). Include at least one labeling strategy besides label-for (aria-label, aria-labelledby, glued spans, placeholder-only). Layout: fieldset stacks with legend-only section titles. Avoid generic centered SaaS card as the only structure. High tier: include shadow DOM host OR iframe apply shell OR wizard/conditional reveal with hidden fields. Mirror structural messiness like these references: Collapsible section animation; Radio styled as buttons; Meter self assessment.
